title: [InstCombine] Detect different vscales in div by shift combine.
summary:
The developers generalized an optimization pattern by extending it from a specific target intrinsic (`llvm.aarch64.sve.cntd`) to a broader class of operations involving `vscale`. The original case folded `cntd(31)` to a constant based on known `vscale` ranges. The generalization recognizes that `vscale` itself is a scalable constant that can participate in arithmetic identities, similar to a regular integer value.

The key insight was that `vscale << Y` and `vscale << Z` share a common base (`vscale`), allowing the existing `foldIDivShl` transformation—which simplifies `(X << Y) / (X << Z)` to `1 << (Y - Z)`—to be extended. The patch modifies the matching logic to also accept `vscale` as the base operand in left shifts, enabling the same simplification for expressions like `(vscale << 2) / (vscale << 1)`.

This approach demonstrates a reusable strategy: identify a core algebraic pattern, then relax the operand matching conditions to include semantically equivalent but syntactically different values (like `vscale` instead of a generic `Value`). This allows a single, well-tested transformation to cover more cases without duplicating logic, improving maintainability and optimization coverage for scalable vector architectures.
